one_sentence: One retriever cannot serve all query types; classify the query and route first, retrieve second, and always define a fallback for the unrouteable query.

operation_candidates.name: EnumerateQueryTypes
operation_candidates.trigger: A single endpoint fronts heterogeneous queries; no query-type table exists.
action: Sample >=50 real queries.
Label each by kind: summarize / lookup / compute-aggregate / compare-multihop / out-of-scope.
Map each kind to its correct handler + index primitive.
operation_candidates.output: Query-type -> handler spec table driving both handler build and router design.
evidence: llamaindex: Stage 0 query distribution; Stage 4 heterogeneity table

operation_candidates.name: BuildPerTypeHandler
operation_candidates.trigger: Type table exists; handlers not yet built.
action: Implement one engine per type (SummaryIndex digest, VectorStoreIndex lookup, NL2SQL compute, SubQuestionQueryEngine compare).
Test each handler against its own query kind in isolation.
Author each handler's destination description / class label.
operation_candidates.output: N independently-verified handlers, each carrying a routing description.

operation_candidates.name: KeywordRouter
operation_candidates.trigger: Destinations are lexically distinct; latency/cost-critical path.
action: regex/substring/heuristic rules over the query -> handler id.
Explicit default for no-match.
No model call.
operation_candidates.output: Sub-millisecond, deterministic, free routing for separable cases.
evidence: langgraph: conditional edge as pure predicate (add_conditional_edges)

operation_candidates.name: EmbeddingRouter
operation_candidates.trigger: Destinations semantically (not lexically) distinct; no per-call LLM budget.
action: Embed the query.
Cosine-match against pre-embedded destination descriptions / few examples per route.
Pick argmax; threshold for fallback.
operation_candidates.output: Cheap low-latency semantic routing without an LLM hop.

operation_candidates.name: LLMSelectorRouter
operation_candidates.trigger: Disambiguation needs reasoning, or a query maps to multiple handlers.
action: Use an LLM selector (single or multi) reading the query + each handler's description.
RouterQueryEngine(selector=LLMSingleSelector.from_defaults(), query_engine_tools=[...]).
Return chosen id(s) + reasoning.
operation_candidates.output: Highest-accuracy routing incl. multi-route fan-out; costs one LLM round-trip.

operation_candidates.name: ConfidenceThresholdFallback
operation_candidates.trigger: Any router that can be uncertain (LLM/embedding) on ambiguous queries.
action: Read the selector's score.
Below threshold -> route to documented default (broad vector index) or explicit refuse path.
Never best-guess silently.
operation_candidates.output: Unrouteable queries land somewhere safe and auditable.

operation_candidates.name: RouteDecisionLogging
action: Log {ts, query_hash, chosen_handler, selector_score, fell_back} on every decision.
Surface a misroute dashboard.
operation_candidates.output: Misroutes become observable metrics, not user-reported anecdotes.
evidence: langgraph: LangSmith trace of the conditional edge

operation_candidates.name: DescriptionFirstDebug
operation_candidates.trigger: Router misroutes a known query class.
action: Before swapping router type, rewrite destination description / add 1-2 disambiguating examples.
Re-evaluate on a labeled routing eval set.
operation_candidates.output: Most misroutes fixed at the description layer with no model change.
evidence: llamaindex: Dilemma 3 'the only signal the router sees'

dilemmas.name: Router misroutes: improve classifier or add fallback?
dilemmas.resolution: Both, in order: diagnose via log -> fix descriptions (OP-08) -> add fallback (OP-06) -> only then consider a bigger selector.

dilemmas.name: LLM router latency vs cheap keyword router
dilemmas.resolution: Tier the router: cheap keyword/embedding front handles the confident majority; LLM selector handles only the ambiguous residual.

dilemmas.name: Route to one handler vs multi-select fan-out
dilemmas.resolution: Single-select by default; multi-select only on the measured multi-intent slice, paired with explicit synthesis.

anti_patterns: A1 router when one index suffices
A3 LLM selector on every query when keyword would do
A6 routing by tenant/permission instead of query kind
A9 tuning the router before the handlers work
boundaries: B1 one index/tool answers everything -> no router
B3 fixed downstream sequence -> static edge
B4 hard real-time + LLM-only correct router -> tier and escalate rarely
B5 agentic/iterative loop -> langgraph cycles, not a one-shot router
